summary: User made 47 UPI transactions totalling ₹2.3L within 8 minutes targeting 3 food delivery merchants.
pattern: Rapid repeated small-value UPI transfers to a cluster of merchants, all within a narrow time window. Transaction intervals averaged 10 seconds.
indicators: txn_count_5m > 20, amounts between ₹100-499, same merchant cluster, consistent device and IP
outcome: Account blocked. ₹2.1L recovered. User confirmed compromised credentials via SIM swap.
recommendation: Block immediately. Initiate SIM swap verification. Notify account holder via registered email.

summary: A single savings account was accessed from 6 distinct device fingerprints in 12 minutes, each initiating a wallet transfer.
pattern: Attacker used automated tooling to cycle through multiple emulated device IDs, bypassing single-device session limits.
indicators: unique_devices_5m >= 5, wallet transfers, varying IP subnets, amounts ₹5K-₹50K
outcome: ₹1.8L transferred before detection. Two transactions reversed. Legal case filed.
recommendation: Step-up authentication after 2nd unique device in session. Require OTP re-verification for each new device.

summary: User transacted in Mumbai at 14:30 IST and then in London at 14:55 IST — 25 minutes apart, physically impossible travel.
pattern: Account credentials stolen and used internationally while the legitimate user was active domestically. Classic account takeover pattern.
indicators: geo_distance_km > 6000 within 30 minutes, international IP, high-value netbanking transfer
outcome: International transaction blocked automatically. Account frozen. Fraud confirmed.
recommendation: Auto-block transactions from geographically impossible locations. Apply 24-hour cooling period for international transfers on compromised accounts.

summary: Colluding user and merchant executed 18 pay-then-refund cycles over 2 hours to extract ₹4.5L from a promotional cashback scheme.
pattern: User pays merchant → merchant issues refund → user claims cashback from platform. Repeated until limit hit. Merchant was a shell company.
indicators: refund_count_1hr > 8, same merchant_id repeated, transaction_type alternating PAYMENT/REFUND, cashback claims
outcome: Merchant account frozen. Cashback reversed. User KYC escalated to RBI.
recommendation: Limit refunds to 3 per merchant per day per user. Flag merchant accounts with refund rates > 10%.

summary: Automated bot network generated 12,000 micro-transactions of exactly ₹1 each to test stolen card numbers for validity.
pattern: Classic card-testing attack. Round-number amounts, zero geolocation, consistent internal IP, sub-second transaction intervals.
indicators: amount == round number, geo_lat=0, geo_lon=0, ip_address internal range, interval < 1 second
outcome: 2,847 valid cards identified before detection. All flagged accounts notified. IP range blocked.
recommendation: CAPTCHA on payment initiation after 3 failed attempts. Rate-limit API to 1 transaction/second per IP.

summary: Network of 23 mule accounts each receiving ₹9,800 deposits from a common source account, totalling ₹2.25L, all within 45 minutes.
pattern: Structuring attack — amounts deliberately kept below ₹10,000 to evade mandatory reporting thresholds. Funds moved onward within 2 hours.
indicators: amount 9000-9999, multiple rapid deposits to distinct accounts from single source, immediate onward transfers
outcome: Source account blocked. All 23 mule accounts frozen per PMLA Section 3. STR filed with FIU-IND.
recommendation: Apply graph-based analysis to detect fan-out patterns from a single source. File STR for structuring patterns per RBI Master Circular.

summary: Dormant account (last used 6 months ago) initiated a ₹3.8L NEFT transfer at 03:17 IST to a newly registered beneficiary.
pattern: Account takeover followed by off-hours large transfer to a beneficiary added in the same session. Classic ATO + fund drainage.
indicators: hour_of_day in [1,2,3,4], amount > ₹50,000, new beneficiary added same session, dormant account
outcome: Transfer intercepted at NEFT clearing. Account frozen. Password reset forced.
recommendation: Mandatory 4-hour cooling period for new beneficiaries. Block large transfers from dormant accounts between 00:00–06:00 IST.

summary: Newly onboarded merchant processed ₹28L in 3 hours before any legitimate product/service was verified. Merchant KYC was forged.
pattern: Fraudster onboarded a fake merchant, routed payments through it from compromised user accounts, then attempted settlement withdrawal.
indicators: merchant age < 7 days, transaction_volume spike > 500% of category baseline, no historical chargebacks yet
outcome: Settlement frozen. Merchant account deactivated. FIR filed.
recommendation: Hold settlements for new merchants for 7 days. Cap daily transaction volume at ₹1L for first 30 days.
